task_completion.description: Read the thermal calibration request document
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor, document viewer, or terminal command (e.g., cat, less) displaying the contents of ~/Documents/thermal_calibration_request.txt.
Camera control software (e.g., KStars/Ekos) or a capture script showing the CCD temperature setpoint at 0°C, exposure time set to 60 seconds, and frame type set to 'Dark'.
Camera control software or a capture script showing the CCD temperature setpoint at -10°C, exposure time set to 60 seconds, and frame type set to 'Dark'.
Camera control software or a capture script showing the CCD temperature setpoint at -20°C, exposure time set to 60 seconds, and frame type set to 'Dark'.

task_completion.description: Save captured frames in the correct subdirectories
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or terminal listing showing the directory ~/Calibration/thermal/ with subdirectories '0C', 'minus10C', and 'minus20C', each containing exactly 3 captured image files (e.g., .fits).

task_completion.description: Create a summary report confirming the successful setpoints
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal command showing the creation and content of ~/Documents/thermal_report.txt, mentioning the successful captures at 0°C, -10°C, and -20°C.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually interacted with the CCD camera simulator
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent must use camera control software (like KStars/Ekos) or an INDI Python script to capture the frames. It should not simply use 'touch' or 'cp' to create fake files in the target directories.

integrity.description: Verify the captured files are genuine image files
integrity.visual_evidence: If file sizes are visible in a file manager or terminal (e.g., via 'ls -l'), the captured frames should have realistic file sizes for images (greater than 0 bytes), indicating they were generated by the camera simulator.

integrity.description: Verify the summary report was genuinely authored
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should be seen typing the report in a text editor or using echo commands, rather than copying a pre-existing solution file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires capturing exactly 3 dark frames of 60 seconds each at three specific temperatures: 0°C, -10°C, and -20°C (with a tolerance of 1.5°C). The files must be saved in the base directory /home/ga/Calibration/thermal/ under the exact subdirectory names '0C', 'minus10C', and 'minus20C'. Finally, a summary report must be saved at /home/ga/Documents/thermal_report.txt.
